// 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/server/server.ts
.all("/*", async (c) => {
  const response = await proxy(`https://app.opencode.ai${path}`, { ... })
  response.headers.set("Content-Security-Policy", csp(hash))
  return response
})

README / docs

// 140_opencode/packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/server.rs
pub fn spawn_local_server(
    app: AppHandle, hostname: String, port: u32, password: String,
) -> (CommandChild, HealthCheck) {
    let (child, exit) = cli::serve(&app, &hostname, port, &password);
    let health_check = HealthCheck(tokio::spawn(async move {
        loop {
            tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)).await;
            if check_health(&url, Some(&password)).await { return Ok(()); }
        }
    }));
    (child, health_check)
}

README / docs

graph LR
    subgraph "packages"
        APP["@opencode-ai/app<br/>(SolidJS UI)"]
        UI["@opencode-ai/ui<br/>(테마·컴포넌트)"]
        SDK["@opencode-ai/sdk"]
    end
    subgraph "shells"
        TAURI["desktop (Tauri v2)"]
        ELECTRON["desktop-electron"]
    end
    TAURI --> APP
    ELECTRON --> APP
    APP --> UI
    APP --> SDK
    SDK -->|HTTP| SERVER["opencode serve"]

Main analysis

App Server · Client Surface — OpenCode

Analysis snapshot: 141_opencode-current/ — app/server/client surfaces.

1. Architecture overview

OpenCode's client surface is a single HTTP server + multiple clients structure. When the core package opencode installs a Hono-based REST+SSE server, the SDK (@opencode-ai/sdk) provides an OpenAPI code generation client, and TUI, desktop, and web UI attach to it through the same HTTP boundary. Like Codex's app-server-protocol, a separate wire schema layer is not extracted. Instead, when the Zod schema is declared in the Hono route, hono-openapi automatically generates the OpenAPI 3.1 specification, and @hey-api/openapi-ts is a one-stop pipeline that extracts a type-safe SDK.

graph TD
    A["opencode core (Bun + Hono)"] --> B["REST API + SSE"]
    B --> C["@opencode-ai/sdk (코드생성)"]
    C --> D["TUI (Ink)"]
    C --> E["desktop — Tauri"]
    C --> F["desktop — Electron"]
    C --> G["web app (SolidJS)"]
    B --> H["app.opencode.ai (reverse proxy)"]
    A --> I["OpenAPI 3.1 spec"]
    I --> C

Here's a summary of the key differences:

Comparison Items Codex (Rust) OpenCode (TypeScript)
Protocol Layer Separate app-server-protocol crate Inlining Zod schema into Hono route
transport stdio + WebSocket HTTP + SSE (+ WebSocket for PTY)
Create Client Manual Rust facade OpenAPI → @hey-api/openapi-ts code generation
Desktop None Tauri + Electron double wrapper
SDK version management v1 only v1 + v2 (workspace support)

2. Server runtime

2.1 Hono app creation and bootstrapping

The server entry point is Server.createApp, opens HTTP with Bun.serve and optionally publishes mDNS.

// 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/server/server.ts
export function listen(opts: {
  port: number
  hostname: string
  mdns?: boolean
  mdnsDomain?: string
  cors?: string[]
}) {
  const app = createApp(opts)
  const server = Bun.serve({ hostname: opts.hostname, port, fetch: app.fetch, websocket })
  if (opts.mdns) MDNS.publish(server.port!, opts.mdnsDomain)
  return server
}
  • CORS: Automatically allow localhost, 127.0.0.1, tauri://localhost, *.opencode.ai
  • Authentication: Basic Auth is applied if the OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD environment variable is present.
  • Workspace Routing: In every request, determine the project directory with x-opencode-directory or ?directory= and wrap it in Instance.provide context.

2.2 Middleware Pipeline

OPTIONS bypass → Basic Auth → request logger → CORS
  → workspace context binding → WorkspaceRouterMiddleware → routes

Instance.provide creates a per-project isolated context for each request. This pattern allows one server process to serve multiple projects simultaneously.

3. API Surface Map

3.1 Route Group

group mount path Core operation file
Global /global health, event, sync-event, config, dispose, upgrade routes/global.ts
Session /session list, create, get, delete, fork, prompt, prompt_async, abort, share, messages routes/session.ts
Event /event subscribe (SSE) routes/event.ts
Project /project list, current, initGit, update routes/project.ts
Provider /provider list, auth, oauth/authorize, oauth/callback routes/provider.ts
Config /config get, update, providers routes/config.ts
MCP /mcp status, add, connect, disconnect, auth/* routes/mcp.ts
File /find, /file text, files, symbols, list, read, status routes/file.ts
PTY /pty list, create, get, update, remove, connect (WS) routes/pty.ts
Permission /permission list, reply routes/permission.ts
Question /question list, reply, reject routes/question.ts
TUI /tui appendPrompt, submitPrompt, executeCommand, selectSession routes/tui.ts
Experimental /experimental tool.ids, tool.list, workspace/*, worktree/*, resource.list routes/experimental.ts
Top / auth.set/remove, instance.dispose, path, vcs, agent, skill, lsp, formatter, command, log server.ts
proxy /* Reverse proxy unmatched paths to app.opencode.ai server.ts

3.2 Endpoints to note

Session Prompt — Synchronous and Asynchronous Dual Path

// 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/server/routes/session.ts
// 동기: 응답을 stream으로 반환
.post("/:sessionID/message", ..., async (c) => {
  return stream(c, async (stream) => {
    const msg = await SessionPrompt.prompt({ ...body, sessionID })
    stream.write(JSON.stringify(msg))
  })
})

// 비동기: 즉시 204 반환, 결과는 SSE로 전달
.post("/:sessionID/prompt_async", ..., async (c) => {
  c.status(204)
  return stream(c, async () => {
    SessionPrompt.prompt({ ...body, sessionID }).catch(...)
  })
})

The synchronous method is used when waiting for results directly from CLI or SDK, and the asynchronous method is used when receiving real-time updates through SSE events from TUI/Web.

PTY WebSocket

// 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/server/routes/pty.ts
.get("/:ptyID/connect", upgradeWebSocket(async (c) => {
  // Bun WebSocket upgrade → bidirectional PTY I/O
  return {
    async onOpen(_event, ws) {
      handler = await Pty.connect(id, ws.raw, cursor)
    },
    onMessage(event) { handler?.onMessage(event.data) },
    onClose()       { handler?.onClose() },
  }
}))

It is the only surface that uses WebSockets. All remaining real-time communications are SSE.

4. SSE event system

4.1 Event Bus Architecture

OpenCode declares type-safe events with BusEvent.define and subscribes globally with Bus.subscribeAll. The SSE endpoint bridges this bus and passes it to the client.

// 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/bus/bus-event.ts
export function define<Type extends string, Properties extends ZodType>(
  type: Type, properties: Properties
) {
  const result = { type, properties }
  registry.set(type, result)
  return result
}

4.2 3 types of SSE channels

Channel path scope Use
instance event GET /event Current Project Session, message, file change, etc.
Global Events GET /global/event full server Instance creation/termination, upgrade
Global Sync Event GET /global/sync-event full server Versioned synchronization events

4.3 Main event types

session.updated          session.error           session.diff
session.status.updated   session.status.idle     session.compacted
message.part.delta       permission.asked        permission.replied
question.asked           question.replied        question.rejected
pty.created              pty.updated             pty.exited         pty.deleted
mcp.tools_changed        project.updated         lsp.updated
file.edited              file.watcher.updated
todo.updated             vcs.branch.updated
server.connected         server.heartbeat        global.disposed
installation.updated     installation.update_available
worktree.ready           worktree.failed
workspace.ready          workspace.failed
command.executed

Heartbeats are sent every 10 seconds to prevent proxy stream stalls.

// 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/server/routes/event.ts
const heartbeat = setInterval(() => {
  q.push(JSON.stringify({ type: "server.heartbeat", properties: {} }))
}, 10_000)

5. SDK package (@opencode-ai/sdk)

5.1 Structure

The SDK is a type-safe client whose code is generated from the OpenAPI specification. Both versions v1 and v2 coexist.

packages/sdk/js/
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts          # createOpencode (server + client 한 번에)
│   ├── client.ts         # createOpencodeClient
│   ├── server.ts         # createOpencodeServer, createOpencodeTui
│   ├── gen/              # 코드생성 결과물
│   │   ├── sdk.gen.ts    # OpencodeClient 클래스
│   │   ├── types.gen.ts  # 요청/응답 타입
│   │   └── client/       # HTTP 클라이언트 기반
│   └── v2/               # 워크스페이스 지원 추가된 v2
│       ├── client.ts
│       ├── server.ts
│       └── gen/
└── package.json          # exports: ".", "./client", "./server", "./v2", "./v2/client"

5.2 Creating a client

// 140_opencode/packages/sdk/js/src/client.ts
export function createOpencodeClient(config?: Config & { directory?: string }) {
  if (config?.directory) {
    config.headers = {
      ...config.headers,
      "x-opencode-directory": encodeURIComponent(config.directory),
    }
  }
  const client = createClient(config)
  return new OpencodeClient({ client })
}

If you pass the directory option, the x-opencode-directory header is automatically attached. The v2 client additionally receives experimental_workspaceID and sets the x-opencode-workspace header.

5.3 Server Launcher

The SDK includes a server launcher that spawns the opencode serve CLI.

// 140_opencode/packages/sdk/js/src/server.ts
export async function createOpencodeServer(options?: ServerOptions) {
  const proc = spawn("opencode", ["serve", `--hostname=${hostname}`, `--port=${port}`], {
    env: { ...process.env, OPENCODE_CONFIG_CONTENT: JSON.stringify(config ?? {}) },
  })
  // stdout에서 "opencode server listening on http://..." 파싱
  return { url, close() { proc.kill() } }
}

createOpencode는 server + client를 한 번에 만드는 편의 함수다.

// 140_opencode/packages/sdk/js/src/index.ts
export async function createOpencode(options?: ServerOptions) {
  const server = await createOpencodeServer(options)
  const client = createOpencodeClient({ baseUrl: server.url })
  return { client, server }
}

5.4 TUI Launcher

export function createOpencodeTui(options?: TuiOptions) {
  const proc = spawn("opencode", args, { stdio: "inherit", ... })
  return { close() { proc.kill() } }
}

6. Web package (@opencode-ai/web)

6.1 역할과 스택

packages/web is a documentation site. It is a combination of Astro + Starlight + SolidJS and is distributed on Cloudflare. Supports i18n in 18 languages.

// 140_opencode/packages/web/astro.config.mjs
export default defineConfig({
  site: config.url,
  base: "/docs",
  output: "server",
  adapter: cloudflare({ imageService: "passthrough" }),
  integrations: [solidJs(), starlight({ title: "OpenCode", ... })],
})

When building, the config schema is extracted from packages/opencode as JSON and included in the document.

6.2 Proxy structure

Reverse proxy routes that do not match the server's last all("/*") route to app.opencode.ai. This allows desktop apps to access the web UI through the local server.

// 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/server/server.ts
.all("/*", async (c) => {
  const response = await proxy(`https://app.opencode.ai${path}`, { ... })
  response.headers.set("Content-Security-Policy", csp(hash))
  return response
})

7. Desktop Wrapper

7.1 Tauri Desktop (packages/desktop)

It is based on Tauri v2. Run opencode serve with the Rust sidecar, wait for the health check, and then launch the SolidJS app in WebView.

// 140_opencode/packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/server.rs
pub fn spawn_local_server(
    app: AppHandle, hostname: String, port: u32, password: String,
) -> (CommandChild, HealthCheck) {
    let (child, exit) = cli::serve(&app, &hostname, port, &password);
    let health_check = HealthCheck(tokio::spawn(async move {
        loop {
            tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)).await;
            if check_health(&url, Some(&password)).await { return Ok(()); }
        }
    }));
    (child, health_check)
}

Tauri plugins: clipboard, deep-link, dialog, opener, os, notification, process, shell, store, updater, http, window-state

7.2 Electron Desktop (packages/desktop-electron)

It is based on Electron + electron-vite. It shares the same @opencode-ai/app UI package as the Tauri version and is packaged with electron-builder. Connect to the local server through the same SDK client.

7.3 Shared App UI (packages/app)

It is a common UI library based on SolidJS. Both Tauri desktop and Electron desktop depend on this package. Playwright-based E2E testing is included.

graph LR
    subgraph "packages"
        APP["@opencode-ai/app<br/>(SolidJS UI)"]
        UI["@opencode-ai/ui<br/>(테마·컴포넌트)"]
        SDK["@opencode-ai/sdk"]
    end
    subgraph "shells"
        TAURI["desktop (Tauri v2)"]
        ELECTRON["desktop-electron"]
    end
    TAURI --> APP
    ELECTRON --> APP
    APP --> UI
    APP --> SDK
    SDK -->|HTTP| SERVER["opencode serve"]

8. Cleaning up exposed surfaces

floor Role core files
HTTP Server Bun + Hono REST + SSE + WS packages/140_opencode/src/server/server.ts
route module Hono router separated by domain packages/140_opencode/src/server/routes/*.ts
Event Bus Type safe pub/sub → SSE bridge packages/140_opencode/src/bus/bus-event.ts
OpenAPI specification Automatically generated at runtime packages/sdk/openapi.json
SDK Code Generation HTTP Client packages/sdk/js/src/
App UI SolidJS shared component packages/app/src/
Tauri shell Rust sidecar + WebView packages/desktop/src-tauri/
Electron shell Node.js main + WebView packages/desktop-electron/src/main/
Document Web Astro + Starlight packages/web/

9. Reuse seam

seam Reason
Automatic generation of OpenAPI specifications Just create a Zod schema and document, SDK, and type come out at once
BusEvent type system If you register an event with discriminated union, SSE payload schema is automatically
Instance.provide context Project isolation per request is guaranteed at the server level
@opencode-ai/app shared UI No need to rewrite UI code when changing desktop shell
SDK server launcher Server process management is encapsulated in one createOpencodeServer
reverse proxy pattern Local server provides web UI transparently

10. Combined Risk

risk Description
Bun-only runtime It is tightly coupled to the Bun API, including Bun.serve, bun:ffi, and websocket
Absence of protocol layer Since the Hono route is a wire schema, if you change the server framework, you have to rewrite the contract layer
SSE one-way The client → server direction requires a separate HTTP call. WebSocket redundancy burden
Maintaining Dual Desktops Tauri + Electron must maintain both shells simultaneously
app.opencode.ai proxy dependency The server's catch-all proxies to an external host, causing the web UI to break in offline environments.
SDK v1/v2 coexistence There is a cost of maintaining both versions of the generated code

List of core files

  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/server/server.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/server/event.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/server/error.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/server/projectors.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/server/mdns.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/server/routes/session.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/server/routes/event.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/server/routes/global.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/server/routes/project.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/server/routes/provider.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/server/routes/config.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/server/routes/mcp.ts

  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/bus/bus-event.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/sdk/js/src/index.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/sdk/js/src/client.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/sdk/js/src/server.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/sdk/js/src/v2/client.ts

snapshot_to_current delta

Area Snapshot Current
Axis surface Paths cited in-body APIs/flags evolve per release — re-verify on upgrade
Host delegation Documented in this checkout Binary-only drift for closed-source CLIs

Change history

  • 2026-03-26: Analyzed OpenCode's server/client surface. We organized the Hono REST+SSE server, OpenAPI code generation SDK, dual desktop wrapper (Tauri+Electron), and web document site structure.

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 006_app-server-client-surfaces/06_oc_surface.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

App Server · Client Surface — OpenCode

분석 스냅샷: 141_opencode-current/ — app/server/client surfaces.

1. 아키텍처 총론

OpenCode의 client surface는 단일 HTTP 서버 + 다중 클라이언트 구조다. 코어 패키지 opencode가 Hono 기반 REST+SSE 서버를 올리면, SDK(@opencode-ai/sdk)가 OpenAPI 코드생성 클라이언트를 제공하고, 그 위에 TUI·데스크톱·웹 UI가 동일한 HTTP 경계를 통해 붙는다. Codex의 app-server-protocol처럼 별도 wire schema 계층을 뽑아내지 않았고, 대신 Hono 라우트에 Zod 스키마를 선언하면 hono-openapi가 OpenAPI 3.1 스펙을 자동 생성하고, @hey-api/openapi-ts가 타입 안전 SDK를 뽑아내는 원스톱 파이프라인이다.

graph TD
    A["opencode core (Bun + Hono)"] --> B["REST API + SSE"]
    B --> C["@opencode-ai/sdk (코드생성)"]
    C --> D["TUI (Ink)"]
    C --> E["desktop — Tauri"]
    C --> F["desktop — Electron"]
    C --> G["web app (SolidJS)"]
    B --> H["app.opencode.ai (reverse proxy)"]
    A --> I["OpenAPI 3.1 spec"]
    I --> C

핵심 차이점을 정리하면 이렇다.

비교 항목 Codex (Rust) OpenCode (TypeScript)
프로토콜 계층 별도 app-server-protocol crate Hono 라우트에 Zod 스키마 인라인
transport stdio + WebSocket HTTP + SSE (+ PTY용 WebSocket)
클라이언트 생성 수동 Rust facade OpenAPI → @hey-api/openapi-ts 코드생성
데스크톱 없음 Tauri + Electron 이중 래퍼
SDK 버전 관리 v1 only v1 + v2 (workspace 지원)

2. 서버 런타임

2.1 Hono 앱 생성과 부트스트랩

서버 진입점은 Server.createApp이고, Bun.serve로 HTTP를 열고 선택적으로 mDNS를 publish한다.

// 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/server/server.ts
export function listen(opts: {
  port: number
  hostname: string
  mdns?: boolean
  mdnsDomain?: string
  cors?: string[]
}) {
  const app = createApp(opts)
  const server = Bun.serve({ hostname: opts.hostname, port, fetch: app.fetch, websocket })
  if (opts.mdns) MDNS.publish(server.port!, opts.mdnsDomain)
  return server
}
  • CORS: localhost, 127.0.0.1, tauri://localhost, *.opencode.ai 자동 허용
  • 인증: OPENCODE_SERVER_PASSWORD 환경변수가 있으면 Basic Auth 적용
  • 워크스페이스 라우팅: 모든 요청에서 x-opencode-directory 또는 ?directory= 로 프로젝트 디렉터리를 결정하고, Instance.provide 컨텍스트로 감싼다

2.2 미들웨어 파이프라인

OPTIONS bypass → Basic Auth → request logger → CORS
  → workspace context binding → WorkspaceRouterMiddleware → routes

Instance.provide가 요청마다 프로젝트별 격리 컨텍스트를 만든다. 이 패턴 덕분에 하나의 서버 프로세스가 여러 프로젝트를 동시에 서빙할 수 있다.

3. API Surface 맵

3.1 라우트 그룹

그룹 마운트 경로 핵심 operation 파일
Global /global health, event, sync-event, config, dispose, upgrade routes/global.ts
Session /session list, create, get, delete, fork, prompt, prompt_async, abort, share, messages routes/session.ts
Event /event subscribe (SSE) routes/event.ts
Project /project list, current, initGit, update routes/project.ts
Provider /provider list, auth, oauth/authorize, oauth/callback routes/provider.ts
Config /config get, update, providers routes/config.ts
MCP /mcp status, add, connect, disconnect, auth/* routes/mcp.ts
File /find, /file text, files, symbols, list, read, status routes/file.ts
PTY /pty list, create, get, update, remove, connect (WS) routes/pty.ts
Permission /permission list, reply routes/permission.ts
Question /question list, reply, reject routes/question.ts
TUI /tui appendPrompt, submitPrompt, executeCommand, selectSession routes/tui.ts
Experimental /experimental tool.ids, tool.list, workspace/*, worktree/*, resource.list routes/experimental.ts
최상위 / auth.set/remove, instance.dispose, path, vcs, agent, skill, lsp, formatter, command, log server.ts
프록시 /* 매치되지 않는 경로를 app.opencode.ai로 역방향 프록시 server.ts

3.2 주목할 엔드포인트

세션 프롬프트 — 동기와 비동기 이중 경로

// 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/server/routes/session.ts
// 동기: 응답을 stream으로 반환
.post("/:sessionID/message", ..., async (c) => {
  return stream(c, async (stream) => {
    const msg = await SessionPrompt.prompt({ ...body, sessionID })
    stream.write(JSON.stringify(msg))
  })
})

// 비동기: 즉시 204 반환, 결과는 SSE로 전달
.post("/:sessionID/prompt_async", ..., async (c) => {
  c.status(204)
  return stream(c, async () => {
    SessionPrompt.prompt({ ...body, sessionID }).catch(...)
  })
})

동기 방식은 CLI나 SDK 에서 결과를 직접 기다릴 때, 비동기 방식은 TUI/웹에서 SSE 이벤트로 실시간 업데이트를 받을 때 사용한다.

PTY WebSocket

// 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/server/routes/pty.ts
.get("/:ptyID/connect", upgradeWebSocket(async (c) => {
  // Bun WebSocket upgrade → bidirectional PTY I/O
  return {
    async onOpen(_event, ws) {
      handler = await Pty.connect(id, ws.raw, cursor)
    },
    onMessage(event) { handler?.onMessage(event.data) },
    onClose()       { handler?.onClose() },
  }
}))

유일하게 WebSocket을 쓰는 표면이다. 나머지 실시간 통신은 모두 SSE다.

4. SSE 이벤트 시스템

4.1 이벤트 버스 아키텍처

OpenCode는 BusEvent.define으로 타입 안전 이벤트를 선언하고, Bus.subscribeAll로 전역 구독한다. SSE 엔드포인트가 이 버스를 브릿지해 클라이언트에 전달한다.

// 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/bus/bus-event.ts
export function define<Type extends string, Properties extends ZodType>(
  type: Type, properties: Properties
) {
  const result = { type, properties }
  registry.set(type, result)
  return result
}

4.2 SSE 채널 3종

채널 경로 스코프 용도
인스턴스 이벤트 GET /event 현재 프로젝트 세션·메시지·파일 변경 등
글로벌 이벤트 GET /global/event 전체 서버 인스턴스 생성/종료, 업그레이드
글로벌 싱크 이벤트 GET /global/sync-event 전체 서버 버전 관리된 동기화 이벤트

4.3 주요 이벤트 타입

session.updated          session.error           session.diff
session.status.updated   session.status.idle     session.compacted
message.part.delta       permission.asked        permission.replied
question.asked           question.replied        question.rejected
pty.created              pty.updated             pty.exited         pty.deleted
mcp.tools_changed        project.updated         lsp.updated
file.edited              file.watcher.updated
todo.updated             vcs.branch.updated
server.connected         server.heartbeat        global.disposed
installation.updated     installation.update_available
worktree.ready           worktree.failed
workspace.ready          workspace.failed
command.executed

heartbeat은 10초 간격으로 전송되어 프록시 스트림 스톨을 방지한다.

// 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/server/routes/event.ts
const heartbeat = setInterval(() => {
  q.push(JSON.stringify({ type: "server.heartbeat", properties: {} }))
}, 10_000)

5. SDK 패키지 (@opencode-ai/sdk)

5.1 구조

SDK는 OpenAPI 스펙에서 코드생성된 타입 안전 클라이언트다. v1과 v2 두 버전이 공존한다.

packages/sdk/js/
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts          # createOpencode (server + client 한 번에)
│   ├── client.ts         # createOpencodeClient
│   ├── server.ts         # createOpencodeServer, createOpencodeTui
│   ├── gen/              # 코드생성 결과물
│   │   ├── sdk.gen.ts    # OpencodeClient 클래스
│   │   ├── types.gen.ts  # 요청/응답 타입
│   │   └── client/       # HTTP 클라이언트 기반
│   └── v2/               # 워크스페이스 지원 추가된 v2
│       ├── client.ts
│       ├── server.ts
│       └── gen/
└── package.json          # exports: ".", "./client", "./server", "./v2", "./v2/client"

5.2 클라이언트 생성

// 140_opencode/packages/sdk/js/src/client.ts
export function createOpencodeClient(config?: Config & { directory?: string }) {
  if (config?.directory) {
    config.headers = {
      ...config.headers,
      "x-opencode-directory": encodeURIComponent(config.directory),
    }
  }
  const client = createClient(config)
  return new OpencodeClient({ client })
}

directory 옵션을 넘기면 자동으로 x-opencode-directory 헤더가 붙는다. v2 클라이언트는 추가로 experimental_workspaceID를 받아 x-opencode-workspace 헤더를 세팅한다.

5.3 서버 런처

SDK에는 opencode serve CLI를 spawn하는 서버 런처가 포함돼 있다.

// 140_opencode/packages/sdk/js/src/server.ts
export async function createOpencodeServer(options?: ServerOptions) {
  const proc = spawn("opencode", ["serve", `--hostname=${hostname}`, `--port=${port}`], {
    env: { ...process.env, OPENCODE_CONFIG_CONTENT: JSON.stringify(config ?? {}) },
  })
  // stdout에서 "opencode server listening on http://..." 파싱
  return { url, close() { proc.kill() } }
}

createOpencode는 server + client를 한 번에 만드는 편의 함수다.

// 140_opencode/packages/sdk/js/src/index.ts
export async function createOpencode(options?: ServerOptions) {
  const server = await createOpencodeServer(options)
  const client = createOpencodeClient({ baseUrl: server.url })
  return { client, server }
}

5.4 TUI 런처

export function createOpencodeTui(options?: TuiOptions) {
  const proc = spawn("opencode", args, { stdio: "inherit", ... })
  return { close() { proc.kill() } }
}

6. 웹 패키지 (@opencode-ai/web)

6.1 역할과 스택

packages/web은 문서 사이트다. Astro + Starlight + SolidJS 조합이며, Cloudflare에 배포된다. 18개 언어의 i18n을 지원한다.

// 140_opencode/packages/web/astro.config.mjs
export default defineConfig({
  site: config.url,
  base: "/docs",
  output: "server",
  adapter: cloudflare({ imageService: "passthrough" }),
  integrations: [solidJs(), starlight({ title: "OpenCode", ... })],
})

빌드 시 packages/opencode에서 config 스키마를 JSON으로 추출해 문서에 포함한다.

6.2 프록시 구조

서버의 마지막 all("/*") 라우트가 매치되지 않는 경로를 app.opencode.ai로 역방향 프록시한다. 이를 통해 데스크톱 앱이 로컬 서버를 거쳐 웹 UI에 접근할 수 있다.

// 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/server/server.ts
.all("/*", async (c) => {
  const response = await proxy(`https://app.opencode.ai${path}`, { ... })
  response.headers.set("Content-Security-Policy", csp(hash))
  return response
})

7. 데스크톱 래퍼

7.1 Tauri 데스크톱 (packages/desktop)

Tauri v2 기반이다. Rust 사이드카로 opencode serve를 실행하고 health check를 기다린 뒤, WebView에 SolidJS 앱을 띄운다.

// 140_opencode/packages/desktop/src-tauri/src/server.rs
pub fn spawn_local_server(
    app: AppHandle, hostname: String, port: u32, password: String,
) -> (CommandChild, HealthCheck) {
    let (child, exit) = cli::serve(&app, &hostname, port, &password);
    let health_check = HealthCheck(tokio::spawn(async move {
        loop {
            tokio::time::sleep(Duration::from_millis(100)).await;
            if check_health(&url, Some(&password)).await { return Ok(()); }
        }
    }));
    (child, health_check)
}

Tauri 플러그인: clipboard, deep-link, dialog, opener, os, notification, process, shell, store, updater, http, window-state

7.2 Electron 데스크톱 (packages/desktop-electron)

Electron + electron-vite 기반이다. Tauri 버전과 동일한 @opencode-ai/app UI 패키지를 공유하고, electron-builder로 패키징한다. 같은 SDK 클라이언트를 통해 로컬 서버에 접속한다.

7.3 공유 앱 UI (packages/app)

SolidJS 기반의 공통 UI 라이브러리다. Tauri 데스크톱과 Electron 데스크톱이 모두 이 패키지를 의존한다. Playwright 기반 E2E 테스트가 포함돼 있다.

graph LR
    subgraph "packages"
        APP["@opencode-ai/app<br/>(SolidJS UI)"]
        UI["@opencode-ai/ui<br/>(테마·컴포넌트)"]
        SDK["@opencode-ai/sdk"]
    end
    subgraph "shells"
        TAURI["desktop (Tauri v2)"]
        ELECTRON["desktop-electron"]
    end
    TAURI --> APP
    ELECTRON --> APP
    APP --> UI
    APP --> SDK
    SDK -->|HTTP| SERVER["opencode serve"]

8. 노출 표면 정리

층 역할 핵심 파일
HTTP 서버 Bun + Hono REST + SSE + WS packages/140_opencode/src/server/server.ts
라우트 모듈 도메인별 분리된 Hono 라우터 packages/140_opencode/src/server/routes/*.ts
이벤트 버스 타입 안전 pub/sub → SSE 브릿지 packages/140_opencode/src/bus/bus-event.ts
OpenAPI 스펙 런타임에서 자동 생성 packages/sdk/openapi.json
SDK 코드생성 HTTP 클라이언트 packages/sdk/js/src/
앱 UI SolidJS 공유 컴포넌트 packages/app/src/
Tauri 셸 Rust sidecar + WebView packages/desktop/src-tauri/
Electron 셸 Node.js main + WebView packages/desktop-electron/src/main/
문서 웹 Astro + Starlight packages/web/

9. 재사용 seam

seam 이유
OpenAPI 스펙 자동 생성 Zod 스키마만 작성하면 문서·SDK·타입이 한 번에 나온다
BusEvent 타입 시스템 discriminated union으로 이벤트를 등록하면 SSE 페이로드 스키마까지 자동
Instance.provide 컨텍스트 요청별 프로젝트 격리가 서버 레벨에서 보장된다
@opencode-ai/app 공유 UI 데스크톱 셸을 바꿔도 UI 코드를 재작성할 필요 없다
SDK server launcher createOpencodeServer 하나로 서버 프로세스 관리가 캡슐화돼 있다
역방향 프록시 패턴 로컬 서버가 웹 UI를 투명하게 제공한다

10. 결합 리스크

리스크 설명
Bun 전용 런타임 Bun.serve, bun:ffi, websocket 등 Bun API에 강하게 결합돼 있다
프로토콜 계층 부재 Hono 라우트가 곧 wire schema라서, 서버 프레임워크를 바꾸면 계약층을 다시 짜야 한다
SSE 단방향 클라이언트→서버 방향은 별도 HTTP 호출이 필요하다. WebSocket 이중화 부담
이중 데스크톱 유지 Tauri + Electron 두 셸을 동시에 유지해야 한다
app.opencode.ai 프록시 의존 서버의 catch-all이 외부 호스트로 프록시하므로, 오프라인 환경에서 웹 UI가 깨진다
SDK v1/v2 공존 두 버전의 생성 코드를 모두 유지하는 비용이 있다

snapshot_to_current 델타

Area Snapshot Current

Host delegation Documented in this checkout Binary-only drift for closed-source CLIs

변경 기록

  • 2026-03-26: OpenCode의 서버/클라이언트 표면을 분석했다. Hono REST+SSE 서버, OpenAPI 코드생성 SDK, 이중 데스크톱 래퍼(Tauri+Electron), 웹 문서 사이트 구조를 정리했다.
31

Axis 07: Sandbox & policy

AXIS

007_sandbox-exec-policy/07_oc_sandbox.md

Overview

Axis 07: Sandbox & policy — developer-depth section from the codex corpus. Structural highlights extracted from the analysis:

  • 분석 스냅샷: 141_opencode-current/ — sandbox and execution policy.

  • OpenCode에는 전용 sandbox/ 디렉토리가 없다. 실행 격리는 세 모듈에 분산돼 있다: Project(프로젝트 경계와 sandbox 디렉토리 관리), Permission(룰셋 기반 실행 허가 엔진), Pty(PTY 프로세스 실행). 거기에 Worktree(git worktree 격리)와 Instance(경로 경계 판정)가 보조 레이어로 작동한다. Codex의 OS-레벨 격리(se
  • 핵심 설계 원칙: OS sandbox 없이도, 모든 도구 호출이 Permission.ask()를 거쳐야 실행된다. 도구가 프로젝트 경계 밖 경로에 접근하면 external_directory 권한이 추가로 요구된다. Worktree는 git 레벨에서 작업 공간을 물리적으로 분리한다.
  • graph TD
  • A["model tool call(bash, edit, write …)"] --> B["Instance.containsPath경로 경계 판정"]
  • B -->|경계 밖| C["external_directorypermission ask"]
  • B -->|경계 안| D["tool-specificpermission ask"]
  • C -->|denied| X["DeniedError"]
  • C -->|ask| E["Permission.ask()"]
  • D -->|denied| X
  • D -->|allow| G["프로세스 실행"]
  • D -->|ask| E

Topics covered

    1. 프로젝트 경계 — Project + Instance
  • 1.1 Instance.containsPath — 경계 판정의 핵심
  • 1.2 Project.sandboxes — 다중 sandbox 디렉토리
    1. Permission 시스템 — 실행 게이트
  • 2.1 Ruleset 구조
  • 2.2 evaluate — 룰 매칭 엔진
  • 2.3 Permission.ask — Deferred 기반 비동기 Approval 흐름
  • 2.4 Agent별 Permission 레이어링
    1. BashTool — 셸 명령 실행과 권한 Integration
  • 3.1 실행 흐름
  • 3.2 BashArity — 명령 프리픽스 정규화
  • 3.3 환경 격리
    1. PTY 실행 — 터미널 세션 격리
    1. Worktree — Git 레벨 작업 격리

Source code snippets

README / docs

graph TD
    A["model tool call<br/>(bash, edit, write …)"] --> B["Instance.containsPath<br/>경로 경계 판정"]
    B -->|경계 밖| C["external_directory<br/>permission ask"]
    B -->|경계 안| D["tool-specific<br/>permission ask"]
    C -->|denied| X["DeniedError"]
    C -->|ask| E["Permission.ask()"]
    D -->|denied| X
    D -->|allow| G["프로세스 실행"]
    D -->|ask| E
    E -->|user approves<br/>'once'| G
    E -->|user approves<br/>'always'| F["ruleset에 추가"]
    F --> G
    E -->|reject| Y["RejectedError"]
    G --> H["child_process.spawn<br/>또는 bun-pty"]
    H --> I["Instance 컨텍스트<br/>안에서 실행"]

    style X fill:#f66,color:#fff
    style Y fill:#f66,color:#fff

README / docs

// project/instance.ts
containsPath(filepath: string) {
  if (Filesystem.contains(Instance.directory, filepath)) return true
  // Non-git projects set worktree to "/" which would match ANY absolute path.
  if (Instance.worktree === "/") return false
  return Filesystem.contains(Instance.worktree, filepath)
},

README / docs

// util/filesystem.ts
export function contains(parent: string, child: string) {
  return !relative(parent, child).startsWith("..")
}

README / docs

// project/project.ts — fromDirectory
if (data.sandbox !== result.worktree && !result.sandboxes.includes(data.sandbox))
  result.sandboxes.push(data.sandbox)

// 존재하지 않는 sandbox 디렉토리는 자동 정리
result.sandboxes = yield* Effect.forEach(
  result.sandboxes,
  (s) => fsys.exists(s).pipe(Effect.orDie, Effect.map((exists) => (exists ? s : undefined))),
  { concurrency: "unbounded" },
).pipe(Effect.map((arr) => arr.filter((x): x is string => x !== undefined)))

README / docs

// project/project.sql.ts
sandboxes: text({ mode: "json" }).notNull().$type<string[]>(),

README / docs

// permission/index.ts
export const Rule = z.object({
  permission: z.string(),   // "bash", "edit", "external_directory" 등
  pattern: z.string(),       // wildcard 패턴
  action: z.enum(["allow", "deny", "ask"]),
})

README / docs

// permission/evaluate.ts
export function evaluate(permission: string, pattern: string, ...rulesets: Rule[][]): Rule {
  const rules = rulesets.flat()
  const match = rules.findLast(
    (rule) => Wildcard.match(permission, rule.permission) && Wildcard.match(pattern, rule.pattern),
  )
  return match ?? { action: "ask", permission, pattern: "*" }
}

README / docs

// util/wildcard.ts — match()
let escaped = pattern
  .replace(/[.+^${}()|[\]\\]/g, "\\$&")
  .replace(/\*/g, ".*")
  .replace(/\?/g, ".")
// "ls *" → "ls" and "ls -la" 모두 매치 (trailing " *" → optional)
if (escaped.endsWith(" .*")) {
  escaped = escaped.slice(0, -3) + "( .*)?"
}

README / docs

// permission/index.ts — ask()
const ask = Effect.fn("Permission.ask")(function* (input) {
  const { approved, pending } = yield* InstanceState.get(state)
  for (const pattern of request.patterns) {
    const rule = evaluate(request.permission, pattern, ruleset, approved)
    if (rule.action === "deny")
      return yield* new DeniedError({ ruleset: ... })
    if (rule.action === "allow") continue
    needsAsk = true
  }
  if (!needsAsk) return

  const deferred = yield* Deferred.make<void, RejectedError | CorrectedError>()
  pending.set(id, { info, deferred })
  void Bus.publish(Event.Asked, info)
  return yield* Deferred.await(deferred)  // ← 사용자 응답까지 블로킹
})

README / docs

// permission/index.ts — reply()
for (const [id, item] of pending.entries()) {
  if (item.info.sessionID !== existing.info.sessionID) continue
  const ok = item.info.patterns.every(
    (pattern) => evaluate(item.info.permission, pattern, approved).action === "allow",
  )
  if (!ok) continue
  pending.delete(id)
  yield* Deferred.succeed(item.deferred, undefined)
}

README / docs

graph LR
    A["defaults<br/>(read: allow, .env: ask)"] --> B["agent-specific<br/>(explore: edit deny)"]
    B --> C["user config<br/>(permission in config.json)"]
    C --> D["runtime approved<br/>(always 응답 누적)"]
    D --> E["evaluate()<br/>findLast 매칭"]

README / docs

// agent/agent.ts
explore: {
  permission: Permission.merge(
    defaults,
    Permission.fromConfig({
      "*": "deny",
      grep: "allow", glob: "allow", list: "allow",
      bash: "allow", read: "allow",
      // edit는 deny — 탐색 전용
    }),
    user,
  ),
},
plan: {
  permission: Permission.merge(
    defaults,
    Permission.fromConfig({
      edit: { "*": "deny", ".opencode/plans/*.md": "allow" },
    }),
    user,
  ),
},

README / docs

sequenceDiagram
    participant M as Model
    participant B as BashTool
    participant P as Permission
    participant S as child_process

    M->>B: execute({ command, workdir })
    B->>B: tree-sitter parse(command)
    B->>B: extract commands + paths
    
    alt cwd 또는 path가 경계 밖
        B->>P: ask(external_directory, [glob patterns])
        P-->>B: allow / reject
    end
    
    alt 명령 패턴 존재
        B->>P: ask(bash, [command patterns])
        P-->>B: allow / reject
    end
    
    B->>S: spawn(command, { shell, cwd, env })
    S-->>B: stdout + stderr + exitCode
    B-->>M: { output, metadata }

README / docs

// permission/arity.ts
export function prefix(tokens: string[]) {
  for (let len = tokens.length; len > 0; len--) {
    const prefix = tokens.slice(0, len).join(" ")
    const arity = ARITY[prefix]
    if (arity !== undefined) return tokens.slice(0, arity)
  }
  if (tokens.length === 0) return []
  return tokens.slice(0, 1)
}

// bash.ts에서 사용
always.add(BashArity.prefix(command).join(" ") + " *")

README / docs

// tool/bash.ts
const shellEnv = await Plugin.trigger("shell.env", { cwd, sessionID, callID }, { env: {} })
const proc = spawn(params.command, {
  shell,           // Shell.acceptable() — fish/nu 제외
  cwd,             // workdir 파라미터 또는 Instance.directory
  env: { ...process.env, ...shellEnv.env },
  stdio: ["ignore", "pipe", "pipe"],
  detached: process.platform !== "win32",
})

README / docs

// shell/shell.ts
process.kill(-pid, "SIGTERM")  // 프로세스 그룹에 SIGTERM
await sleep(200)
if (!exited()) process.kill(-pid, "SIGKILL")

README / docs

// pty/index.ts — create()
const spawn = await pty()  // bun-pty
const proc = spawn(command, args, {
  name: "xterm-256color",
  cwd,         // state.dir — Instance 디렉토리
  env: {
    ...process.env,
    ...shellEnv.env,
    TERM: "xterm-256color",
    OPENCODE_TERMINAL: "1",
  },
})

README / docs

yield* Effect.addFinalizer(() =>
  Effect.sync(() => {
    for (const session of state.sessions.values()) {
      teardown(session)  // session.process.kill()
    }
  }),
)

README / docs

// worktree/index.ts
const setup = Effect.fnUntraced(function* (info: Info) {
  // 1. git worktree add
  yield* git(["worktree", "add", "--no-checkout", "-b", info.branch, info.directory], {
    cwd: Instance.worktree,
  })
  // 2. Project sandbox 목록에 등록
  yield* Effect.promise(() =>
    Project.addSandbox(Instance.project.id, info.directory)
  )
})

README / docs

const branch = `140_opencode/${name}`
const directory = pathSvc.join(root, name)
// 디렉토리 존재 + 브랜치 존재 모두 확인
if (yield* fsys.exists(directory)) continue
const branchCheck = yield* git(["show-ref", "--verify", "--quiet", ref])
if (branchCheck.code === 0) continue

README / docs

yield* stopFsmonitor(entry.path)
yield* git(["worktree", "remove", "--force", entry.path])
yield* cleanDirectory(entry.path)
yield* git(["branch", "-D", branch])

README / docs

yield* git(["reset", "--hard", base.ref], { cwd: worktreePath })
yield* sweep(worktreePath)  // git clean -ffdx + failed removes 재시도
yield* git(["submodule", "update", "--init", "--recursive", "--force"])

README / docs

// tool/external-directory.ts
export async function assertExternalDirectory(ctx, target, options) {
  if (Instance.containsPath(target)) return  // 경계 안이면 통과
  const parentDir = kind === "directory" ? target : path.dirname(target)
  const glob = path.join(parentDir, "*")
  await ctx.ask({
    permission: "external_directory",
    patterns: [glob],
    always: [glob],
    metadata: { filepath: target, parentDir },
  })
}

README / docs

// tool/bash.ts
if (["cd", "rm", "cp", "mv", "mkdir", ...].includes(command[0])) {
  for (const arg of command.slice(1)) {
    const resolved = await fs.realpath(path.resolve(cwd, arg))
    if (!Instance.containsPath(normalized)) {
      directories.add(dir)
    }
  }
}

Main analysis

OpenCode sandbox and execution policy — distributed implementation analysis

Analysis snapshot: 141_opencode-current/ — sandbox and execution policy.

OpenCode does not have a dedicated sandbox/ directory. Execution isolation is distributed across three modules: Project (manages project boundaries and sandbox directories), Permission (ruleset-based execution permission engine), and Pty (executes PTY processes). In addition, Worktree (git worktree isolation) and Instance (path boundary determination) act as auxiliary layers. Unlike Codex's OS-level isolation (seatbelt/landlock), OpenCode controls execution with application-level permission gates.

Core design principle: Even without an OS sandbox, all tool calls must go through Permission.ask() to be executed. If a tool accesses a path outside the project boundary, additional external_directory permission is required. Worktree physically separates workspaces at the git level.

graph TD
    A["model tool call<br/>(bash, edit, write …)"] --> B["Instance.containsPath<br/>경로 경계 판정"]
    B -->|경계 밖| C["external_directory<br/>permission ask"]
    B -->|경계 안| D["tool-specific<br/>permission ask"]
    C -->|denied| X["DeniedError"]
    C -->|ask| E["Permission.ask()"]
    D -->|denied| X
    D -->|allow| G["프로세스 실행"]
    D -->|ask| E
    E -->|user approves<br/>'once'| G
    E -->|user approves<br/>'always'| F["ruleset에 추가"]
    F --> G
    E -->|reject| Y["RejectedError"]
    G --> H["child_process.spawn<br/>또는 bun-pty"]
    H --> I["Instance 컨텍스트<br/>안에서 실행"]

    style X fill:#f66,color:#fff
    style Y fill:#f66,color:#fff

1. Project boundary — Project + Instance

Unlike Codex's OS sandbox, which blocks the file system at the kernel level, OpenCode determines boundaries through path comparison.

1.1 Instance.containsPath — The key to boundary determination

// project/instance.ts
containsPath(filepath: string) {
  if (Filesystem.contains(Instance.directory, filepath)) return true
  // Non-git projects set worktree to "/" which would match ANY absolute path.
  if (Instance.worktree === "/") return false
  return Filesystem.contains(Instance.worktree, filepath)
},

Filesystem.contains only checks for the presence of the .. prefix in path.relative():

// util/filesystem.ts
export function contains(parent: string, child: string) {
  return !relative(parent, child).startsWith("..")
}

This decision acts as the gatekeeper to all file manipulation tools, including BashTool, WriteTool, and EditTool.

1.2 Project.sandboxes — Multiple sandbox directories

The Project entity has a field sandboxes: string[]. This is added every time the worktree is created:

// project/project.ts — fromDirectory
if (data.sandbox !== result.worktree && !result.sandboxes.includes(data.sandbox))
  result.sandboxes.push(data.sandbox)

// 존재하지 않는 sandbox 디렉토리는 자동 정리
result.sandboxes = yield* Effect.forEach(
  result.sandboxes,
  (s) => fsys.exists(s).pipe(Effect.orDie, Effect.map((exists) => (exists ? s : undefined))),
  { concurrency: "unbounded" },
).pipe(Effect.map((arr) => arr.filter((x): x is string => x !== undefined)))

In the DB schema, sandboxes are stored as a JSON array:

// project/project.sql.ts
sandboxes: text({ mode: "json" }).notNull().$type<string[]>(),
concept Codex OpenCode
Boundary decision OS kernel (seatbelt/landlock) Filesystem.contains() path comparison
sandbox directory single (process working directory) multiple (Project.sandboxes[])
network isolation CODEX_SANDBOX_NETWORK_DISABLED environment variable None

2. Permission system — execution gate

It is the core of OpenCode's execution control. It corresponds to Codex's Starlark exec-policy engine, but the implementation is completely different.

2.1 Ruleset structure

// permission/index.ts
export const Rule = z.object({
  permission: z.string(),   // "bash", "edit", "external_directory" 등
  pattern: z.string(),       // wildcard 패턴
  action: z.enum(["allow", "deny", "ask"]),
})

Permission categories defined in Config:

Category target default
bash Run shell command ask
edit Write/Edit File ask
read Read file allow (except .env*)
external_directory Access outside project boundaries ask
task Run subagent ask
glob, grep, list Navigation Tools allow
webfetch, websearch Web Access Varies by agent
skill Skill Execution ask

2.2 evaluate — Rule matching engine

// permission/evaluate.ts
export function evaluate(permission: string, pattern: string, ...rulesets: Rule[][]): Rule {
  const rules = rulesets.flat()
  const match = rules.findLast(
    (rule) => Wildcard.match(permission, rule.permission) && Wildcard.match(pattern, rule.pattern),
  )
  return match ?? { action: "ask", permission, pattern: "*" }
}

findLast semantics: Rules added later override previous rules. This means that in the structure of building rules using Permission.merge() → flat(), those merged later take precedence.

Wildcard matching is based on regular expressions that convert * → .*, ? → .:

// util/wildcard.ts — match()
let escaped = pattern
  .replace(/[.+^${}()|[\]\\]/g, "\\$&")
  .replace(/\*/g, ".*")
  .replace(/\?/g, ".")
// "ls *" → "ls" and "ls -la" 모두 매치 (trailing " *" → optional)
if (escaped.endsWith(" .*")) {
  escaped = escaped.slice(0, -3) + "( .*)?"
}

2.3 Permission.ask — Deferred-based asynchronous permission flow

// permission/index.ts — ask()
const ask = Effect.fn("Permission.ask")(function* (input) {
  const { approved, pending } = yield* InstanceState.get(state)
  for (const pattern of request.patterns) {
    const rule = evaluate(request.permission, pattern, ruleset, approved)
    if (rule.action === "deny")
      return yield* new DeniedError({ ruleset: ... })
    if (rule.action === "allow") continue
    needsAsk = true
  }
  if (!needsAsk) return

  const deferred = yield* Deferred.make<void, RejectedError | CorrectedError>()
  pending.set(id, { info, deferred })
  void Bus.publish(Event.Asked, info)
  return yield* Deferred.await(deferred)  // ← 사용자 응답까지 블로킹
})

cooperative blocking implemented as Deferred in Effect: Tool execution stops and waits until the user responds with once/always/reject in the UI.

When responding with always, other pending requests for the same session are automatically resolved:

// permission/index.ts — reply()
for (const [id, item] of pending.entries()) {
  if (item.info.sessionID !== existing.info.sessionID) continue
  const ok = item.info.patterns.every(
    (pattern) => evaluate(item.info.permission, pattern, approved).action === "allow",
  )
  if (!ok) continue
  pending.delete(id)
  yield* Deferred.succeed(item.deferred, undefined)
}

2.4 Permission layering by agent

graph LR
    A["defaults<br/>(read: allow, .env: ask)"] --> B["agent-specific<br/>(explore: edit deny)"]
    B --> C["user config<br/>(permission in config.json)"]
    C --> D["runtime approved<br/>(always 응답 누적)"]
    D --> E["evaluate()<br/>findLast 매칭"]

Each agent has a different rights profile:

// agent/agent.ts
explore: {
  permission: Permission.merge(
    defaults,
    Permission.fromConfig({
      "*": "deny",
      grep: "allow", glob: "allow", list: "allow",
      bash: "allow", read: "allow",
      // edit는 deny — 탐색 전용
    }),
    user,
  ),
},
plan: {
  permission: Permission.merge(
    defaults,
    Permission.fromConfig({
      edit: { "*": "deny", ".opencode/plans/*.md": "allow" },
    }),
    user,
  ),
},

3. BashTool — Unifies shell command execution and permissions

BashTool has the most complex execution path in OpenCode. Parse the command with tree-sitter, and perform a separate permission check for each path and command pattern.

3.1 Execution flow

sequenceDiagram
    participant M as Model
    participant B as BashTool
    participant P as Permission
    participant S as child_process

    M->>B: execute({ command, workdir })
    B->>B: tree-sitter parse(command)
    B->>B: extract commands + paths
    
    alt cwd 또는 path가 경계 밖
        B->>P: ask(external_directory, [glob patterns])
        P-->>B: allow / reject
    end
    
    alt 명령 패턴 존재
        B->>P: ask(bash, [command patterns])
        P-->>B: allow / reject
    end
    
    B->>S: spawn(command, { shell, cwd, env })
    S-->>B: stdout + stderr + exitCode
    B-->>M: { output, metadata }

3.2 BashArity — Command prefix normalization

Normalize commands for permission pattern matching. Convert npm run dev to npm run dev * and git checkout main to git checkout *:

// permission/arity.ts
export function prefix(tokens: string[]) {
  for (let len = tokens.length; len > 0; len--) {
    const prefix = tokens.slice(0, len).join(" ")
    const arity = ARITY[prefix]
    if (arity !== undefined) return tokens.slice(0, arity)
  }
  if (tokens.length === 0) return []
  return tokens.slice(0, 1)
}

// bash.ts에서 사용
always.add(BashArity.prefix(command).join(" ") + " *")

There are 160+ command prefixes defined in the ARITY dictionary. Examples: git: 2, npm run: 3, docker compose: 3.

3.3 Environmental isolation

// tool/bash.ts
const shellEnv = await Plugin.trigger("shell.env", { cwd, sessionID, callID }, { env: {} })
const proc = spawn(params.command, {
  shell,           // Shell.acceptable() — fish/nu 제외
  cwd,             // workdir 파라미터 또는 Instance.directory
  env: { ...process.env, ...shellEnv.env },
  stdio: ["ignore", "pipe", "pipe"],
  detached: process.platform !== "win32",
})

In Unix, separate process groups with detached: true and kill each process group with Shell.killTree():

// shell/shell.ts
process.kill(-pid, "SIGTERM")  // 프로세스 그룹에 SIGTERM
await sleep(200)
if (!exited()) process.kill(-pid, "SIGKILL")

4. Run PTY — Isolate terminal session

The Pty module manages interactive terminal sessions. Unlike BashTool, it is a terminal controlled directly by the user without permission check.

// pty/index.ts — create()
const spawn = await pty()  // bun-pty
const proc = spawn(command, args, {
  name: "xterm-256color",
  cwd,         // state.dir — Instance 디렉토리
  env: {
    ...process.env,
    ...shellEnv.env,
    TERM: "xterm-256color",
    OPENCODE_TERMINAL: "1",
  },
})
Characteristics BashTool Pty
How it runs child_process.spawn bun-pty
Permission check Yes (bash + external_directory) None (direct user control)
output control stdout/stderr pipe → buffering WebSocket Streaming
life cycle command unit Per-session (InstanceState management)
buffer MAX_METADATA_LENGTH (30KB) BUFFER_LIMIT (2MB)

A PTY session is bound to an instance with InstanceState. When an instance is terminated, all PTY processes are also killed:

yield* Effect.addFinalizer(() =>
  Effect.sync(() => {
    for (const session of state.sessions.values()) {
      teardown(session)  // session.process.kill()
    }
  }),
)

5. Worktree — Git level task isolation

Worktree is OpenCode's unique isolation mechanism. Create an independent working directory using git worktree and register it in the project's sandbox list.

5.1 Creation flow

// worktree/index.ts
const setup = Effect.fnUntraced(function* (info: Info) {
  // 1. git worktree add
  yield* git(["worktree", "add", "--no-checkout", "-b", info.branch, info.directory], {
    cwd: Instance.worktree,
  })
  // 2. Project sandbox 목록에 등록
  yield* Effect.promise(() =>
    Project.addSandbox(Instance.project.id, info.directory)
  )
})

The Worktree branch follows the naming 140_opencode/{slug} and is created after checking for duplicates:

const branch = `140_opencode/${name}`
const directory = pathSvc.join(root, name)
// 디렉토리 존재 + 브랜치 존재 모두 확인
if (yield* fsys.exists(directory)) continue
const branchCheck = yield* git(["show-ref", "--verify", "--quiet", ref])
if (branchCheck.code === 0) continue

5.2 Cleanup and reset

When removing a worktree, stop the fsmonitor daemon → delete the worktree → delete the branch.

yield* stopFsmonitor(entry.path)
yield* git(["worktree", "remove", "--force", entry.path])
yield* cleanDirectory(entry.path)
yield* git(["branch", "-D", branch])

Reset to the default branch with --hard and then clean with git clean -ffdx:

yield* git(["reset", "--hard", base.ref], { cwd: worktreePath })
yield* sweep(worktreePath)  // git clean -ffdx + failed removes 재시도
yield* git(["submodule", "update", "--init", "--recursive", "--force"])

6. External Directory — Out-of-bounds access control

When accessing files outside the project boundary, a separate permission layer operates:

// tool/external-directory.ts
export async function assertExternalDirectory(ctx, target, options) {
  if (Instance.containsPath(target)) return  // 경계 안이면 통과
  const parentDir = kind === "directory" ? target : path.dirname(target)
  const glob = path.join(parentDir, "*")
  await ctx.ask({
    permission: "external_directory",
    patterns: [glob],
    always: [glob],
    metadata: { filepath: target, parentDir },
  })
}

BashTool additionally parses arguments such as cd, rm, and cp to interpret the path:

// tool/bash.ts
if (["cd", "rm", "cp", "mv", "mkdir", ...].includes(command[0])) {
  for (const arg of command.slice(1)) {
    const resolved = await fs.realpath(path.resolve(cwd, arg))
    if (!Instance.containsPath(normalized)) {
      directories.add(dir)
    }
  }
}

Technical Reference

Codex vs OpenCode execution policy comparison

perspective Codex OpenCode
isolation level OS kernel (seatbelt/landlock) Compare application paths
rule language Starlark DSL (.rules file) JSON config + wildcard pattern
rule matching ExecPolicyManager evaluate() + findLast
Approved Model Stage 5 (Never~Granular) Step 3 (allow/deny/ask)
network isolation Blocking based on environment variables None
Work Isolation process sandbox git worktree
Command classification is_known_safe_command + command_might_be_dangerous BashArity prefix + wildcard matching

Core module map

module Role file
Instance Path boundary determination, context management project/instance.ts
Project sandbox directory management, worktree connection project/project.ts
Permission Ruleset evaluation, deferred approval flow permission/index.ts, permission/evaluate.ts
BashTool Shell command parsing, double permission check tool/bash.ts
Pty PTY Terminal Session Management pty/index.ts
Worktree git worktree isolation worktree/index.ts
BashArity Command prefix normalization (160+ entries) permission/arity.ts
Shell shell detection, process tree exit shell/shell.ts
Wildcard pattern matching engine util/wildcard.ts
Filesystem.contains Determination of path inclusion util/filesystem.ts

Fork Perspective

  • The Permission system is neatly isolated with Effect ServiceMap, making it easy to reuse.
  • BashArity dictionary (160+ commands) can be retrieved independently
  • The combination evaluate() + Wildcard.match() is simple and predictable.
  • Worktree isolation provides strong separation of tasks in git-based projects.
  • There is no OS-level isolation, so defenses against malicious commands are weak.
  • No network isolation — additional layers are needed in sensitive environments
  • Filesystem.contains() may be vulnerable to symlink traversal (realpath not used)

List of core files

  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/project/instance.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/project/project.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/project/project.sql.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/permission/index.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/permission/evaluate.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/permission/arity.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/permission/schema.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/tool/bash.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/tool/tool.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/tool/external-directory.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/tool/write.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/pty/index.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/worktree/index.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/shell/shell.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/util/wildcard.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/util/filesystem.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/agent/agent.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/config/config.ts

Previous article: [[07_co_sandbox]]
Next article: [[08_oc_config]]

snapshot_to_current delta

Area Snapshot Current
Axis surface Paths cited in-body APIs/flags evolve per release — re-verify on upgrade
Host delegation Documented in this checkout Binary-only drift for closed-source CLIs

Change history

  • 2025-07-15: Analyzed distributed implementation without dedicated sandbox directory. Permission system, BashTool execution flow, Worktree isolation, and PTY session management were organized.

Korean corpus (reference)

Verbatim KO corpus 007_sandbox-exec-policy/07_oc_sandbox.md for cross-check; code blocks unchanged.

OpenCode 샌드박스와 실행 정책 — 분산 구현 분석

분석 스냅샷: 141_opencode-current/ — sandbox and execution policy.

OpenCode에는 전용 sandbox/ 디렉토리가 없다. 실행 격리는 세 모듈에 분산돼 있다: Project(프로젝트 경계와 sandbox 디렉토리 관리), Permission(룰셋 기반 실행 허가 엔진), Pty(PTY 프로세스 실행). 거기에 Worktree(git worktree 격리)와 Instance(경로 경계 판정)가 보조 레이어로 작동한다. Codex의 OS-레벨 격리(seatbelt/landlock)와 달리, OpenCode는 애플리케이션-레벨 권한 게이트로 실행을 제어한다.

핵심 설계 원칙: OS sandbox 없이도, 모든 도구 호출이 Permission.ask()를 거쳐야 실행된다. 도구가 프로젝트 경계 밖 경로에 접근하면 external_directory 권한이 추가로 요구된다. Worktree는 git 레벨에서 작업 공간을 물리적으로 분리한다.

graph TD
    A["model tool call<br/>(bash, edit, write …)"] --> B["Instance.containsPath<br/>경로 경계 판정"]
    B -->|경계 밖| C["external_directory<br/>permission ask"]
    B -->|경계 안| D["tool-specific<br/>permission ask"]
    C -->|denied| X["DeniedError"]
    C -->|ask| E["Permission.ask()"]
    D -->|denied| X
    D -->|allow| G["프로세스 실행"]
    D -->|ask| E
    E -->|user approves<br/>'once'| G
    E -->|user approves<br/>'always'| F["ruleset에 추가"]
    F --> G
    E -->|reject| Y["RejectedError"]
    G --> H["child_process.spawn<br/>또는 bun-pty"]
    H --> I["Instance 컨텍스트<br/>안에서 실행"]

    style X fill:#f66,color:#fff
    style Y fill:#f66,color:#fff

1. 프로젝트 경계 — Project + Instance

Codex의 OS sandbox가 파일시스템을 커널 레벨에서 차단하는 것과 달리, OpenCode는 경로 비교로 경계를 판정한다.

1.1 Instance.containsPath — 경계 판정의 핵심

// project/instance.ts
containsPath(filepath: string) {
  if (Filesystem.contains(Instance.directory, filepath)) return true
  // Non-git projects set worktree to "/" which would match ANY absolute path.
  if (Instance.worktree === "/") return false
  return Filesystem.contains(Instance.worktree, filepath)
},

Filesystem.contains는 path.relative()의 .. 접두사 여부만 확인한다:

// util/filesystem.ts
export function contains(parent: string, child: string) {
  return !relative(parent, child).startsWith("..")
}

이 판정은 BashTool, WriteTool, EditTool 등 모든 파일 조작 도구에서 게이트키퍼 역할을 한다.

1.2 Project.sandboxes — 다중 sandbox 디렉토리

Project 엔티티에는 sandboxes: string[] 필드가 있다. 이것은 worktree가 생성될 때마다 추가된다:

// project/project.ts — fromDirectory
if (data.sandbox !== result.worktree && !result.sandboxes.includes(data.sandbox))
  result.sandboxes.push(data.sandbox)

// 존재하지 않는 sandbox 디렉토리는 자동 정리
result.sandboxes = yield* Effect.forEach(
  result.sandboxes,
  (s) => fsys.exists(s).pipe(Effect.orDie, Effect.map((exists) => (exists ? s : undefined))),
  { concurrency: "unbounded" },
).pipe(Effect.map((arr) => arr.filter((x): x is string => x !== undefined)))

DB 스키마에서 sandboxes는 JSON 배열로 저장된다:

// project/project.sql.ts
sandboxes: text({ mode: "json" }).notNull().$type<string[]>(),
개념 Codex OpenCode
경계 판정 OS 커널 (seatbelt/landlock) Filesystem.contains() 경로 비교
sandbox 디렉토리 단일 (프로세스 작업 디렉토리) 다중 (Project.sandboxes[])
네트워크 격리 CODEX_SANDBOX_NETWORK_DISABLED 환경변수 없음

2. Permission 시스템 — 실행 게이트

OpenCode의 실행 제어 핵심이다. Codex의 Starlark exec-policy 엔진과 대응하지만, 구현은 완전히 다르다.

2.1 Ruleset 구조

// permission/index.ts
export const Rule = z.object({
  permission: z.string(),   // "bash", "edit", "external_directory" 등
  pattern: z.string(),       // wildcard 패턴
  action: z.enum(["allow", "deny", "ask"]),
})

Config에서 정의하는 permission 카테고리:

카테고리 대상 기본값
bash 셸 명령 실행 ask
edit 파일 쓰기/수정 ask
read 파일 읽기 allow (.env* 제외)
external_directory 프로젝트 경계 밖 접근 ask
task 서브에이전트 실행 ask
glob, grep, list 탐색 도구 allow
webfetch, websearch 웹 접근 에이전트별 상이
skill 스킬 실행 ask

2.2 evaluate — 룰 매칭 엔진

// permission/evaluate.ts
export function evaluate(permission: string, pattern: string, ...rulesets: Rule[][]): Rule {
  const rules = rulesets.flat()
  const match = rules.findLast(
    (rule) => Wildcard.match(permission, rule.permission) && Wildcard.match(pattern, rule.pattern),
  )
  return match ?? { action: "ask", permission, pattern: "*" }
}

findLast 시맨틱: 뒤에 추가된 규칙이 앞선 규칙을 오버라이드한다. 이것은 Permission.merge() → flat()으로 규칙을 쌓는 구조에서 나중에 merge된 것이 우선한다는 의미다.

Wildcard 매칭은 * → .*, ? → .으로 변환하는 정규식 기반이다:

// util/wildcard.ts — match()
let escaped = pattern
  .replace(/[.+^${}()|[\]\\]/g, "\\$&")
  .replace(/\*/g, ".*")
  .replace(/\?/g, ".")
// "ls *" → "ls" and "ls -la" 모두 매치 (trailing " *" → optional)
if (escaped.endsWith(" .*")) {
  escaped = escaped.slice(0, -3) + "( .*)?"
}

2.3 Permission.ask — Deferred 기반 비동기 승인 흐름

// permission/index.ts — ask()
const ask = Effect.fn("Permission.ask")(function* (input) {
  const { approved, pending } = yield* InstanceState.get(state)
  for (const pattern of request.patterns) {
    const rule = evaluate(request.permission, pattern, ruleset, approved)
    if (rule.action === "deny")
      return yield* new DeniedError({ ruleset: ... })
    if (rule.action === "allow") continue
    needsAsk = true
  }
  if (!needsAsk) return

  const deferred = yield* Deferred.make<void, RejectedError | CorrectedError>()
  pending.set(id, { info, deferred })
  void Bus.publish(Event.Asked, info)
  return yield* Deferred.await(deferred)  // ← 사용자 응답까지 블로킹
})

Effect의 Deferred로 구현된 cooperative blocking: 도구 실행이 멈추고, 사용자가 UI에서 once/always/reject로 응답할 때까지 대기한다.

always 응답 시 같은 세션의 대기 중인 다른 요청도 자동 해소된다:

// permission/index.ts — reply()
for (const [id, item] of pending.entries()) {
  if (item.info.sessionID !== existing.info.sessionID) continue
  const ok = item.info.patterns.every(
    (pattern) => evaluate(item.info.permission, pattern, approved).action === "allow",
  )
  if (!ok) continue
  pending.delete(id)
  yield* Deferred.succeed(item.deferred, undefined)
}

2.4 에이전트별 Permission 레이어링

graph LR
    A["defaults<br/>(read: allow, .env: ask)"] --> B["agent-specific<br/>(explore: edit deny)"]
    B --> C["user config<br/>(permission in config.json)"]
    C --> D["runtime approved<br/>(always 응답 누적)"]
    D --> E["evaluate()<br/>findLast 매칭"]

에이전트마다 다른 권한 프로파일을 가진다:

// agent/agent.ts
explore: {
  permission: Permission.merge(
    defaults,
    Permission.fromConfig({
      "*": "deny",
      grep: "allow", glob: "allow", list: "allow",
      bash: "allow", read: "allow",
      // edit는 deny — 탐색 전용
    }),
    user,
  ),
},
plan: {
  permission: Permission.merge(
    defaults,
    Permission.fromConfig({
      edit: { "*": "deny", ".opencode/plans/*.md": "allow" },
    }),
    user,
  ),
},

3. BashTool — 셸 명령 실행과 권한 통합

BashTool은 OpenCode에서 가장 복잡한 실행 경로를 가진다. tree-sitter로 명령을 파싱하고, 경로와 명령 패턴 각각에 대해 별도 permission check를 수행한다.

3.1 실행 흐름

sequenceDiagram
    participant M as Model
    participant B as BashTool
    participant P as Permission
    participant S as child_process

    M->>B: execute({ command, workdir })
    B->>B: tree-sitter parse(command)
    B->>B: extract commands + paths
    
    alt cwd 또는 path가 경계 밖
        B->>P: ask(external_directory, [glob patterns])
        P-->>B: allow / reject
    end
    
    alt 명령 패턴 존재
        B->>P: ask(bash, [command patterns])
        P-->>B: allow / reject
    end
    
    B->>S: spawn(command, { shell, cwd, env })
    S-->>B: stdout + stderr + exitCode
    B-->>M: { output, metadata }

3.2 BashArity — 명령 프리픽스 정규화

Permission 패턴 매칭을 위해 명령을 정규화한다. npm run dev를 npm run dev *로, git checkout main을 git checkout *로 변환한다:

// permission/arity.ts
export function prefix(tokens: string[]) {
  for (let len = tokens.length; len > 0; len--) {
    const prefix = tokens.slice(0, len).join(" ")
    const arity = ARITY[prefix]
    if (arity !== undefined) return tokens.slice(0, arity)
  }
  if (tokens.length === 0) return []
  return tokens.slice(0, 1)
}

// bash.ts에서 사용
always.add(BashArity.prefix(command).join(" ") + " *")

ARITY 딕셔너리에는 160+개의 명령 프리픽스가 정의돼 있다. 예: git: 2, npm run: 3, docker compose: 3.

3.3 환경 격리

// tool/bash.ts
const shellEnv = await Plugin.trigger("shell.env", { cwd, sessionID, callID }, { env: {} })
const proc = spawn(params.command, {
  shell,           // Shell.acceptable() — fish/nu 제외
  cwd,             // workdir 파라미터 또는 Instance.directory
  env: { ...process.env, ...shellEnv.env },
  stdio: ["ignore", "pipe", "pipe"],
  detached: process.platform !== "win32",
})

Unix에서 detached: true로 프로세스 그룹을 분리하고, Shell.killTree()로 프로세스 그룹 단위 종료한다:

// shell/shell.ts
process.kill(-pid, "SIGTERM")  // 프로세스 그룹에 SIGTERM
await sleep(200)
if (!exited()) process.kill(-pid, "SIGKILL")

4. PTY 실행 — 터미널 세션 격리

Pty 모듈은 대화형 터미널 세션을 관리한다. BashTool과 달리 permission check 없이 사용자가 직접 제어하는 터미널이다.

// pty/index.ts — create()
const spawn = await pty()  // bun-pty
const proc = spawn(command, args, {
  name: "xterm-256color",
  cwd,         // state.dir — Instance 디렉토리
  env: {
    ...process.env,
    ...shellEnv.env,
    TERM: "xterm-256color",
    OPENCODE_TERMINAL: "1",
  },
})
특성 BashTool Pty
실행 방식 child_process.spawn bun-pty
Permission check 있음 (bash + external_directory) 없음 (사용자 직접 제어)
출력 제어 stdout/stderr pipe → 버퍼링 WebSocket 스트리밍
생명주기 명령 단위 세션 단위 (InstanceState 관리)
버퍼 MAX_METADATA_LENGTH (30KB) BUFFER_LIMIT (2MB)

PTY 세션은 InstanceState로 인스턴스에 바인딩된다. 인스턴스 종료 시 모든 PTY 프로세스도 함께 kill된다:

yield* Effect.addFinalizer(() =>
  Effect.sync(() => {
    for (const session of state.sessions.values()) {
      teardown(session)  // session.process.kill()
    }
  }),
)

5. Worktree — Git 레벨 작업 격리

Worktree는 OpenCode 고유의 격리 메커니즘이다. git worktree를 활용해 독립된 작업 디렉토리를 만들고, 이것을 Project의 sandbox 목록에 등록한다.

5.1 생성 흐름

// worktree/index.ts
const setup = Effect.fnUntraced(function* (info: Info) {
  // 1. git worktree add
  yield* git(["worktree", "add", "--no-checkout", "-b", info.branch, info.directory], {
    cwd: Instance.worktree,
  })
  // 2. Project sandbox 목록에 등록
  yield* Effect.promise(() =>
    Project.addSandbox(Instance.project.id, info.directory)
  )
})

Worktree 브랜치는 140_opencode/{slug} 네이밍을 따르며, 중복 체크 후 생성한다:

const branch = `140_opencode/${name}`
const directory = pathSvc.join(root, name)
// 디렉토리 존재 + 브랜치 존재 모두 확인
if (yield* fsys.exists(directory)) continue
const branchCheck = yield* git(["show-ref", "--verify", "--quiet", ref])
if (branchCheck.code === 0) continue

5.2 정리와 리셋

Worktree 제거 시 fsmonitor 데몬 중지 → worktree 삭제 → 브랜치 삭제 순서:

yield* stopFsmonitor(entry.path)
yield* git(["worktree", "remove", "--force", entry.path])
yield* cleanDirectory(entry.path)
yield* git(["branch", "-D", branch])

리셋은 기본 브랜치로 --hard 리셋 후 git clean -ffdx로 클리닝:

yield* git(["reset", "--hard", base.ref], { cwd: worktreePath })
yield* sweep(worktreePath)  // git clean -ffdx + failed removes 재시도
yield* git(["submodule", "update", "--init", "--recursive", "--force"])

6. External Directory — 경계 밖 접근 제어

프로젝트 경계 밖 파일 접근 시 별도 permission 레이어가 작동한다:

// tool/external-directory.ts
export async function assertExternalDirectory(ctx, target, options) {
  if (Instance.containsPath(target)) return  // 경계 안이면 통과
  const parentDir = kind === "directory" ? target : path.dirname(target)
  const glob = path.join(parentDir, "*")
  await ctx.ask({
    permission: "external_directory",
    patterns: [glob],
    always: [glob],
    metadata: { filepath: target, parentDir },
  })
}

BashTool에서는 추가로 cd, rm, cp 등의 인자를 파싱해서 경로를 해석한다:

// tool/bash.ts
if (["cd", "rm", "cp", "mv", "mkdir", ...].includes(command[0])) {
  for (const arg of command.slice(1)) {
    const resolved = await fs.realpath(path.resolve(cwd, arg))
    if (!Instance.containsPath(normalized)) {
      directories.add(dir)
    }
  }
}

기술 참조

Codex vs OpenCode 실행 정책 비교

관점 Codex OpenCode
격리 레벨 OS 커널 (seatbelt/landlock) 애플리케이션 경로 비교
규칙 언어 Starlark DSL (.rules 파일) JSON config + wildcard 패턴
규칙 매칭 ExecPolicyManager evaluate() + findLast
승인 모델 5단계 (Never~Granular) 3단계 (allow/deny/ask)
네트워크 격리 환경변수 기반 차단 없음
작업 격리 프로세스 sandbox git worktree
명령 분류 is_known_safe_command + command_might_be_dangerous BashArity prefix + wildcard matching

핵심 모듈 맵

모듈 역할 파일
Instance 경로 경계 판정, 컨텍스트 관리 project/instance.ts
Project sandbox 디렉토리 관리, worktree 연결 project/project.ts
Permission 룰셋 평가, Deferred 승인 흐름 permission/index.ts, permission/evaluate.ts
BashTool 셸 명령 파싱, 이중 permission check tool/bash.ts
Pty PTY 터미널 세션 관리 pty/index.ts
Worktree git worktree 격리 worktree/index.ts
BashArity 명령 프리픽스 정규화 (160+ 엔트리) permission/arity.ts
Shell 셸 탐지, 프로세스 트리 종료 shell/shell.ts
Wildcard 패턴 매칭 엔진 util/wildcard.ts
Filesystem.contains 경로 포함 판정 util/filesystem.ts

포크 관점

  • Permission 시스템은 Effect ServiceMap으로 깔끔하게 격리돼 있어 재사용이 쉽다
  • BashArity 딕셔너리(160+ 명령)는 독립적으로 가져갈 수 있다
  • evaluate() + Wildcard.match() 조합은 단순하고 예측 가능하다
  • Worktree 격리는 git 기반 프로젝트에서 강력한 작업 분리를 제공한다
  • OS-레벨 격리가 없어서 악의적 명령에 대한 방어가 약하다
  • 네트워크 격리가 없다 — 민감한 환경에서는 추가 레이어가 필요하다
  • Filesystem.contains()는 symlink traversal에 취약할 수 있다 (realpath 미사용)

핵심 파일 목록

  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/project/instance.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/project/project.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/project/project.sql.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/permission/index.ts
  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/permission/evaluate.ts

  • 140_opencode/packages/140_opencode/src/config/config.ts
